steps.title: Find the initial value
steps.content: For $h(x)=a\cdot b^x$, plugging in $x=0$ gives $h(0)=a$. The table says $h(0)=1.23$, so $a=1.23$.

steps.title: Find the base from h(2)
steps.content: $h(2)=1.23\cdot b^2=1.54$, so $b^2=\dfrac{1.54}{1.23}\approx 1.252$ and $b\approx \sqrt{1.252}\approx 1.12$. Check $x=4$: $1.23\cdot(1.12)^4\approx 1.23\cdot 1.574\approx 1.94$, which matches the table.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $1.12(0.23)^x$: $h(0)=1.12$, not $1.23$; and $0.23$ would shrink the values. Eliminated.
  • B) $1.12(1.23)^x$: $h(0)=1.12$, not $1.23$. Eliminated.
  • C) $1.23(0.12)^x$: correct starting value, but $h(2)=1.23(0.12)^2\approx 0.018$, nowhere near $1.54$. Eliminated.
  • D) $1.23(1.12)^x$: $h(0)=1.23$, $h(2)\approx 1.54$, $h(4)\approx 1.94$. Matches all rows.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
